title: Mosiera moaensis
taxonomy: Mosiera moaensis is classified as a species within the genus Mosiera. It belongs to the family Myrtaceae, which consists of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. The genus Mosiera is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the Americas. Mosiera moaensis is a shrub or small tree that is endemic to the island of Hispaniola in the Caribbean. It is a member of the order Myrtales, which is a group of flowering plants that includes the myrtle family and other related families.
uses: Mosiera moaensis, a plant native to Hawaii, is reportedly used in traditional medicine.
